customer_anxiety_profile: Malcolm is not anxious — he's passively compliant. He already refused this lead once (prior contact told him a tree needed removal; he declined). He agreed to the appointment without enthusiasm. His show-risk is not anxiety-driven; it's low-commitment-driven. He said 'you can call me back' rather than 'I'll be there.'
unresolved_concerns: Tree removal / trimming: Malcolm's original objection was that he didn't want to remove a tree. He agreed to see the estimate but has not committed to any tree action. If the design requires tree removal or trimming, this objection will resurface at the proposal.

Co-owner / title status: Bucky's co-owner question was malformed ('anyone else in the town'). Malcolm's 'nope' may reflect occupancy, not title status. If a spouse or co-owner is on the title and not present at the appointment, the close is structurally blocked per protocols.spouse_decision.
Appointment ambiguity: Bucky said 'tomorrow or Monday at 3PM' at 08:39 after already confirming Monday at 3PM. The closer should confirm the correct date at the top of the call.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior Ion contact: Malcolm was already contacted by Ion, told a tree needed removal, and declined. He may be mildly skeptical about why he's being called again. The closer should acknowledge the prior contact briefly and frame this appointment as a fresh look with a design in hand.

Tree on property: Malcolm expressed reluctance about tree removal and ambivalence about trimming ('if I'm trimming, I might as well take it down'). Do not lead with tree removal. Let the design surface the shading impact and let Malcolm decide.
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will call Monday at 3PM (phone appointment).
Bucky will send a text reminder after the call.
Bucky will 'get working on your design' before the call.
closer_should_know: Malcolm already said no to this lead once — a prior Ion contact told him a tree needed removal and he declined. Bucky re-opened the door by framing the appointment as 'see the estimate and then decide about the tree.' The closer must honor that frame: lead with the design and let Malcolm decide about the tree based on what he sees. If the closer opens with 'so about that tree,' the appointment is likely over in 60 seconds.
